bonuses.leadin: W.H. Auden’s long poem The Age of Anxiety uses a modified form of this poetic verse, as seen in its opening line: “my deuce, my double, my dear image.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this verse form used to write The Dream of the Rood and most other Old English poetry. It is named for a literary device in which words with the same initial sound appear consecutively.
The Dream of the Rood appears alongside alliterative poems by Cynewulf (“KIH-nih-woolf”) and a group of prose homilies in this Old English literature codex named for an Italian city.

Long before Auden did it, many Middle English poets also revived the use of alliterative verse, such as in this allegorical William Langland poem in which Will searches for salvation through a series of dreams.
answers_sanitized: alliterative verse [or alliterated verse; accept alliteration]
